extraction.one_line_summary: BARGUIL Warren won from a seven-rider breakaway while LEKNESSUND Andreas took pink jersey by finishing second at 28 seconds to EVENEPOEL Remco.
tactical_patterns: LEKNESSUND Andreas prioritized intermediate sprint bonuses (3 seconds) before final climb, deliberately trading stage win for jersey calculation.
Took 80km for breakaway formation despite early attacks, suggesting UAE Emirates chasing ALMEIDA João split exhausted peloton's control capacity.
VAN WILDER Ilan isolated as last EVENEPOEL domestique with 3km remaining, revealing SOUDAL's willingness to cede jersey rather than burn matches.

rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: won from break despite being only prior Grand Tour stage winner in seven-man group, suggesting others marked him unsuccessfully
rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: positive: took pink at 23 by 28 seconds through tactical sprint bonuses and second place, Arctic Circle rider executing mature GC calculus
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: deliberately allowed jersey to go at 2+ minutes, team emptied early suggesting prioritization of later mountain stages
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: caught in early split requiring UAE chase effort, vulnerability on medium-mountain day foreshadows high-mountain concerns
rider_signals.CONCI Nicola: positive: launched first attack on final climb from breakaway, showing ALPECIN aggression even when outnumbered
rider_signals.VAN WILDER Ilan: positive: lasted longest of EVENEPOEL domestiques into final 3km on difficult terrain, emerging as key mountain support
rider_signals.HEALY Ben: neutral: prominent in early attack phase but failed to make final seven-man selection despite multiple attempts
rider_signals.SCOTSON Callum: neutral: active in early breakaway attempts suggesting team hunting stage opportunities on medium-mountain terrain
